description:
You are acting as a release-manager who wants to automate a **very small** packaging
    workflow with GNU make.  
    All work must happen strictly inside the directory  
    /home/user/release  

    Starting point already present in the container:
      • /home/user/release/app/main.py    (a placeholder application file)  
      • /home/user/release/VERSION        (which currently contains exactly the text 1.0.0\n)

    Your job
      1. Create a Makefile in /home/user/release (name must be exactly “Makefile”, capital M).  
      2. Make sure the Makefile contains three phony targets: package, clean, and help.  
      3. The package target must:  
         a. Read the version string from the VERSION file (do NOT hard-code the version).  
         b. Create (if it does not already exist) a sub-directory called build/  
         c. Create a gzipped tarball of the whole app/ directory named:  
                build/app-<version-from-file>.tar.gz  
         d. Print the three status lines **exactly** (character-for-character) and **only** these lines:
                Creating build directory...
                Packaging source into build/app-<version-from-file>.tar.gz
                Done.
            (The test harness will invoke “make -s package”, so use echo statements
             inside the recipe instead of letting make print the commands.)  
      4. The clean target must delete the build/ directory (if it exists) **quietly**, i.e. 
         without producing an error when build/ is absent.  
      5. The help target must list the available targets in one line of plain text:
             Available targets: package clean help

    Verification rules used by the test harness
      • After your session, running the single command  
            cd /home/user/release && make -s package  
        must create the file /home/user/release/build/app-1.0.0.tar.gz  
        and print exactly the three status lines shown above.  
      • Running  
            cd /home/user/release && make -s clean  
        afterwards must remove the /home/user/release/build directory.  
      • Running  
            cd /home/user/release && make -s help  
        must output exactly:  
            Available targets: package clean help

    Do not run make yourself; just author the Makefile so that the automated tests
    can run these commands successfully.

    Note: you do not have root privileges, so avoid sudo or absolute paths outside /home/user.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth data for automated grading.
        Anything in this section will NOT be shown to the agent.
    -->
    <initial_state>
        Directory /home/user/release already exists.

        File /home/user/release/VERSION
        ───────────────────────────────
        1.0.0\n

        File /home/user/release/app/main.py
        ───────────────────────────────────
        print("Hello, World!")\n
    </initial_state>

    <expected_makefile>
        The Makefile must be located at /home/user/release/Makefile

        Mandatory variables / structure (independent of formatting/whitespace):
            VERSION := $(shell cat VERSION)

            .PHONY: package clean help

            package:
                @mkdir -p build
                @echo "Creating build directory..."
                @tar -czf build/app-$(VERSION).tar.gz app > /dev/null
                @echo "Packaging source into build/app-$(VERSION).tar.gz"
                @echo "Done."

            clean:
                @rm -rf build

            help:
                @echo "Available targets: package clean help"
    </expected_makefile>

    <runtime_checks>
        1. After “make -s package”:
           • stdout must be exactly the three lines
                 Creating build directory...
                 Packaging source into build/app-1.0.0.tar.gz
                 Done.
             (ending with a single newline)
           • File /home/user/release/build/app-1.0.0.tar.gz must exist and
             be a valid gzip file (non-zero size).

        2. After “make -s clean”:
           • Directory /home/user/release/build must no longer exist.

        3. “make -s help” prints exactly:
             Available targets: package clean help
    </runtime_checks>
